# Transcrição: The_value_of_systems_and_processes

- **Fonte:** The_value_of_systems_and_processes
- **Duração:** 01:17

---

## [00:00 - 01:17]

My dad and his brothers started one of South Australia's first Asian groceries, Thunyan fact. Mansel Park still there, shout out to TVP. It was interesting because I watched my dad and his brothers, my aunties, they served the business. The business was not serving our family. We were slaves to the business. I watched it ruin their health, rob them of youth, rob them of physical well-being. I just watched that business destroy them. So when I had a business, I go, OK, well default, how can I serve you? And every day I'd lock in at six or seven a.m. Because I was watching too much Gary Vaynerchuk at the time. Work your face off. The reason why I'm bringing it up is because I think because my parents, they didn't understand the power of systems and processes. You know, when you turn your businesses into a McDonald's, you can take anybody in and they can run the business. My dad and his brothers didn't have to be the people in there every single day. They should have worked towards making themselves redundant. What they did was they took a massive loan, took massive risk to create themselves a job. You could hide for that so that he could have more time working on the business. So then when I went into my business, same thing, I did all the admin when I started. I did everything. I couldn't undo that coding until I met you. Until I met you, I kind of went, oh, wow, it's not about being strapped to the desk nine to five. That's not how you add the most value to your business. I didn't understand the value of systems and processes.
